message:
feat(abi): port 17 GLIBC_PRIVATE __nss_* + __internal_*netgrent stubs (bd-p3eqw)

Continue closing the libc-internal NSS surface by stubbing 17
GLIBC_PRIVATE-versioned helpers exported by libc.so.6. None of these
have a stable public ABI; programs that bind to them are tightly
coupled to a specific glibc version anyway, so fail-safe stubs are
fine.

Netgroup iteration (3):
  - __internal_setnetgrent: 0 (failure to set up).
  - __internal_getnetgrent_r: 0 (no entries) + errnop=ENOENT.
  - __internal_endnetgrent: 1 (success ending nothing).

Generic dispatcher helpers (4):
  - __nss_database_get: 0 (no configuration).
  - __nss_disable_nscd: void no-op (no nscd hookup).
  - __nss_hash: returns 0 (constant valid hash).
  - __nss_lookup: -1 (no module/function found).

files-module shared file-handle helpers (5):
  - __nss_files_data_open: NULL.
  - __nss_files_data_setent: -1 (NSS_STATUS_UNAVAIL).
  - __nss_files_data_endent: 1 (NSS_STATUS_SUCCESS, ending nothing).
  - __nss_files_data_put: void no-op.
  - __nss_files_fopen: NULL.

Typed lookup helpers (3) — return -1 with errnop=ENOENT:
  - __nss_group_lookup2 / __nss_passwd_lookup2 / __nss_services_lookup2.

Line-reading helpers (2):
  - __nss_parse_line_result: 0 (skip line / NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND).
  - __nss_readline: -1 (TRYAGAIN).

Tests: 7 abi tests covering the netgroup iteration trio with full
errnop verification, generic dispatcher safety (NULL inputs), files
data handle round-trip, the three typed lookup2 helpers' ENOENT
contract, and the line-reading helpers' return shapes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2o7i","title":"Fix pthread child-thread shared-memory test hang","description":"Workspace test gate stalls in frankenlibc-core on the pthread thread shared-memory unit case. Reproduces as a single-test run and blocks closure work. Need root-cause and deterministic fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"WindyKite","created_at":"2026-02-18T19:56:12.313379972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T21:49:36.176713481Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T21:49:36.176686841Z","close_reason":"Fixed create_thread TID republish race causing join hang; targeted reproducer passes via rch. Workspace gate run completed but failed on unrelated stdio_abi printf_redirection test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pthread","testing","triage"],"comments":[{"id":773,"issue_id":"bd-2o7i","author":"WindyKite","text":"Root cause + patch: create_thread was writing handle.tid in parent after clone return. That late store can race with CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID when child exits quickly and resurrect stale non-zero TID, causing join_thread to wait forever. Patched crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs to remove the parent-side tid.store and rely on CLONE_PARENT_SETTID publication. Validation via rch: cargo fmt --check PASS; cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; targeted reproducer cargo test -p frankenlibc-core child_thread_can_write_shared_memory -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 PASS repeatedly on remote worker. Full cargo test --workspace --all-targets was attempted via rch but rch daemon instability/queue contention produced duplicated long-running jobs (b-6482/b-6494), so full-suite completion evidence is pending queue recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-18T21:48:09Z"}]}

Host coverage 140/2654.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:10:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2poiz","title":"PORT: 9 _nss_dns_* NSS plugin lookup stubs","description":"Add the 9 _nss_dns_* NSS DNS plugin lookup stubs. Pair with the nss_files set/get/end stubs already shipped. Each returns NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND (= 0) and sets *errnop = ENOENT + *h_errnop = HOST_NOT_FOUND (= 1) when non-NULL, so the NSS dispatch layer falls through to the next module. Functions: _nss_dns_getcanonname_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname2_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname3_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyname4_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr_r, _nss_dns_gethostbyaddr2_r, _nss_dns_getnetbyname_r, _nss_dns_getnetbyaddr_r. Signatures match the canonical glibc nss_dns convention so register placement of errnop / h_errnop matches what callers actually pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:49:43.779296239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:49:43.779296239Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Signatures match the canonical glibc nss_dns convention so register placement of errnop / h_errnop matches what callers actually pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:49:43.779296239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:53:34.540006579Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:53:34.537992736Z","close_reason":"shipped: 9 _nss_dns_* lookup stubs (getcanonname + 4 gethostbyname* + 2 gethostbyaddr* + 2 getnetby*) returning NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND + ENOENT + HOST_NOT_FOUND via shared helper + 4 abi tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2pw","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (design + offline artifact format)","description":"Design SOS-derived barrier certificates for runtime admissibility.

Background:
- Barrier certificates give formal invariance: once inside safe set, system stays inside under allowed actions.
- We want heavy SOS/SDP work offline; runtime does cheap polynomial evaluation.

Design tasks:
- Choose 1-2 concrete invariants to start (allocator/quarantine depth, pointer provenance, bootstrap ordering).
- Define state vector x (small, observable at runtime) and safe set S.
- Define polynomial barrier B(x) and rule: allow if B(x) >= 0, deny/repair if B(x) < 0.
- Define artifact format: coefficients + scaling + hash + proof metadata.

Acceptance criteria:
- Clear mapping from invariants to barrier inputs.
- Artifact is versioned and deterministic; runtime verifier is trivial.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.210389959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:37.625758402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:37.625728966Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":775,"issue_id":"bd-2pw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by GentleOwl. Starting design of SOS-derived barrier certificates for runtime admissibility. Will investigate existing barrier.rs, runtime_math decision flow, and identify concrete invariants for allocator/quarantine depth and pointer provenance.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:59Z"},{"id":776,"issue_id":"bd-2pw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Complete (GentleOwl)

Design document: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/sos_barrier_design.md

### Summary of deliverables:

**Two concrete invariants:**

1. **Invariant A: Quarantine Depth Safety Envelope** (legacy anchor: malloc/nptl)
   - State: (depth, contention, adverse_rate, lambda_latency) — 4 vars, normalized [0,1]
   - Degree-3 polynomial barrier B_A(x) with DSOS/SDSOS relaxation
   - Safe set: depth provides sufficient UAF detection within latency budget
   - Cadence-gated (every 256 frees), ~100ns evaluation
   
2. **Invariant B: Pointer Provenance Admissibility** (legacy anchor: elf/dl-*/malloc arena)
   - State: (risk_ppm, validation_depth, bloom_fp_rate, arena_pressure) — 4 vars
   - Degree-2 polynomial in fixed-point ppm arithmetic (NO floats on hot path)
   - Safe set: validation depth adequate for current risk level
   - Hot-path (every decide()), <15ns evaluation

**Artifact format:**
- BarrierCertificate struct: version, name, legacy_anchor, state_indices, num_variables, degree, monomial_exponents, gram_upper, normalization, threshold, proof_hash, evaluation_cadence
- Static const data compiled into binary (no file I/O)
- SHA-256 proof hash verified at init, fallback to predicate barrier on mismatch
- Versioned, deterministic, immutable

**Key constraints met:**
- Clear mapping from invariants to barrier inputs ✓
- Artifact is versioned and deterministic ✓
- Runtime verifier is trivial (quadratic form or expanded polynomial) ✓
- Hot-path budget: <15ns for Invariant B, <100ns cadenced for Invariant A ✓
- Monotone escalation only (barrier violation cannot de-escalate) ✓","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:24:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2r0","title":"Perf budget CI: enforce strict<20ns and hardened<200ns targets on selected hot APIs","description":"Extreme optimization mapping + AGENTS constraints.

Deliverables:
- Add CI checks for hot-path latency budgets by mode.
- Include variance guardrails and repeat-runs policy.

Acceptance:
- Budget breach fails CI with hotspot attribution report.
- Budget exceptions require explicit temporary waiver bead.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): perf_budget_test (8 pass) + gate script. All budget tiers defined, variance guardrails reasonable, waivers validated, hotpath symbols match matrix.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.266579047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Perf budget CI enforcement operational. 8 harness tests pass. check_perf_budget.sh validates budget tiers, variance guardrails, waivers, and hotpath symbol classification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-2bd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-2wp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-4rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r0","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:48Z"},{"id":778,"issue_id":"bd-2ry","author":"RusticCoast","text":"Consolidated closure evidence (RusticCoast):

All bd-2ry dependency subtasks are closed and integrated:
- bd-2xp3: clean-room semantics contract + scope ledger
- bd-ahjd: deterministic nested/edge fixture pack + triage docs
- bd-146t: core phase-1 capture/restore invariant guards + deterministic logs
- bd-24b6: ABI wiring with strict/hardened mode-aware validation + explicit deferred transfer signaling

Implemented strategy outcome for this wave:
1) Behavior is explicit and constrained (not silent):
- capture/validation wired in core+ABI with deterministic guards
- true non-local stack transfer backend remains explicitly deferred and signaled
2) signal-mask interaction handling is explicit in ABI metadata path and fixture/evidence logs
3) no silent host call-through in setjmp ABI path
4) support matrix remains constrained (setjmp-family not advertised as production surface), consistent with deferred transfer backend posture

Deterministic gates/tests passing:
- scripts/check_setjmp_semantics_contract.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_fixture_pack.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_phase1_core.sh
- scripts/check_setjmp_abi_wiring.sh
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_semantics_contract_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_fixture_pack_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_phase1_core_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_abi_wiring_test -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi setjmp_abi::tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core phase1_ -- --nocapture

Primary artifact bundles:
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-2xp3/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-ahjd/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-146t/*
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-24b6/*

Result: bd-2ry deliverables for strategy + constrained semantics + tested behavior are satisfied and evidence-indexed.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:36:25Z"}]}
@@ -1027,6 +1027,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ovcrt","title":"PORT: setmode + getmode (BSD chmod symbolic-mode parser/applier)","description":"BSD setmode(mode_str) parses a chmod-style symbolic mode string ("u+x", "g-w", "a=rwx", "u=rwx,go=rx") into an opaque "bitbox" of operations. Companion getmode(bbox, current_mode) applies the operations to current_mode and returns the new mode. Used by chmod(1), find(1) -perm, install(1), mtree, and many other BSD utilities. The full POSIX 1003.1 grammar covers comma-chained clauses, who-spec (u/g/o/a/empty), op (+/-/=), and perm (rwx + s/t/X + u/g/o-as-perm). For v1 ship the common subset: comma-chained clauses, u/g/o/a/empty who, +/-/= op, r/w/x/s/t perms (skip X conditional and u/g/o-as-perm copy semantics — both are uncommon in real config files). Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::setmode::{parse, apply, ChmodOp} + thin abi shims that allocate the opaque bitbox via malloc.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:29:19.325396211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:37:18.646132252Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:37:18.643708885Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::setmode (parse + apply) + stdlib_abi setmode/getmode with malloc'd magic-header bitbox; 23+11 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-p5i","title":"Drift: Validate decision-law vs decide() implementation (conservative merge rules)","description":"Ensure the documented decision law matches the code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the actual merge order and hard gates in decide().
- Add an invariant test: hard gates (barrier, full_validation_trigger_ppm, repair_trigger_ppm) always dominate soft heuristics.

Why:
- Without this, future kernels may accidentally weaken safety by de-escalation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:13.967421067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:32:15.376378455Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:32:15.376294938Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1132,"issue_id":"bd-p5i","author":"DustyPuma","text":"Decision-law validation complete. Added 4 invariant tests: (1) decide_hard_gate_cascade_order_is_invariant - source-code structure test verifying barrier > full_validation_trigger > repair_trigger > Allow order with no locks in hard-gate region; (2) decide_barrier_non_admissible_always_denies_or_repairs - behavioral test with 512MiB request exceeding both mode limits; (3) decide_high_risk_never_allows - behavioral test with 950k ppm risk; (4) decide_hardened_repair_trigger_never_allows - behavioral test with 200k ppm in hardened. Decision law: risk aggregation (lock-free 45 bonuses) -> design bonus -> profile selection (soft) -> barrier admissibility (HARD) -> action cascade (HARD: !admissible->Deny/Repair, risk>=full_trigger->FullValidate, heals+risk>=repair_trigger->Repair, else Allow). All 4 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:32:04Z"}]}

@@ -9219,6 +9219,179 @@ pub unsafe extern "C" fn _nss_dns_getnetbyaddr_r(
     NSS_STATUS_NOTFOUND
 }
 
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// __internal_*netgrent + __nss_* GLIBC_PRIVATE helpers (17 stubs)
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/// `__internal_setnetgrent(*group, *result) -> int` — would set up
+/// netgroup iteration. Stub returns 0 (failure).
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __internal_setnetgrent(
